_comment: Naming-enforcement hook — DISABLED by default at the plugin level (note the leading underscore on '_PreToolUse'). Middle-option opt-in: (1) users enable this hook globally by renaming '_PreToolUse' → 'PreToolUse' here, and (2) per-project opt-in is gated by project_config.enforce_naming_hook in REGISTRY.yaml. The hook prompt below checks both the file path and the project's enforce_naming_hook flag before blocking.

hooks.prompt: Check whether the file being written is a governed document under a project that has opted into naming enforcement. Steps: (1) Find the nearest REGISTRY.yaml walking up from the target file. (2) If no REGISTRY.yaml is found OR the file's project_config.enforce_naming_hook is not true, respond: {"decision": "approve", "reason": "Project has not opted into naming enforcement."} (3) Otherwise, a governed document is any file in the project's tracked_dirs (typically docs/) with extensions .md, .docx, .pdf, .html, .pptx, .xlsx, .yaml, .json, .sh, excluding infrastructure-exempt filenames listed in naming_rules.infrastructure_exempt. (4) If it IS a governed document, validate the filename against the naming convention: descriptive-name-YYYYMMDD-VX.Y.ext. If the filename does NOT match, respond: {"decision": "block", "reason": "Filename does not follow naming convention. Expected: descriptive-name-YYYYMMDD-VX.Y.ext"}. Otherwise respond: {"decision": "approve", "reason": "OK"}.
